name: Loanstar Title Loans
description_short: LoanStar Title Loans provides car title loans up to $10,000 at multiple Texas locations, requiring only your vehicle, photo ID, and a clear title to qualify.
description_long:
LoanStar Title Loans traces its origins to 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia, positioning itself from the start as a provider of emergency liquidity for vehicle owners. Over three-plus decades the company expanded into Texas, building a network of branches across the Dallas-Fort Worth metro area. The Fort Worth presence includes locations on Camp Bowie, McCar Ave, and E Lancaster Ave within the city, plus additional branches in Haltom City, Richland Hills, and Burleson — giving borrowers in the western and southern suburbs several options for in-person service. The company's stated mission centers on helping working Americans leverage the equity in their vehicles when other credit options are unavailable.

LoanStar's core product is the auto title loan — a short-term secured loan in which the borrower's vehicle title is pledged as collateral. Loan amounts reach up to $10,000, making LoanStar suitable for borrowers who need more than a typical payday or small-dollar lender offers. The application requires three items: the vehicle (which stays with the borrower during the loan), a government-issued photo ID, and a clear, lien-free vehicle title. LoanStar structures repayment in two ways — single installment and multiple installment — and provides publicly disclosed fee schedules for $600, $1,200, and $2,000 loan amounts per Texas OCCC requirements. Accepted payment methods include cash, check, debit card, and Western Union.

Several operational factors distinguish LoanStar from smaller or independent title lenders. The company is licensed and regulated by the Texas Office of Consumer Credit Commissioner, and the site links directly to OCCC-required fee disclosures for both installment types — a level of regulatory compliance that is mandated but not always visible on competitor sites. The multi-location Fort Worth cluster means borrowers across different parts of the metro have a branch within roughly 15 miles. LoanStar also provides an online payment portal, a toll-free national customer service number (877-511-CASH), and a Spanish-language interface option on its website.

Title loans carry significant financial risk that prospective borrowers must weigh carefully. The effective APRs on single-installment title loans are typically in the triple digits, and costs escalate rapidly if a loan is rolled over or extended. The most consequential risk is collateral: default puts the borrower's vehicle at risk of repossession — for many low-income households, both their primary transportation and their access to employment. This product is appropriate only for consumers who have a concrete repayment plan, have exhausted lower-cost alternatives such as credit union PALs or employer advances, and fully understand the lien being placed on their title. The in-person-only application process and limited hours — no Sundays, weekday closing at 6 PM — may also constrain access for hourly workers with inflexible schedules.
pros: Loans available up to $10,000 — higher ceiling than typical payday or small-dollar lenders
Minimal application requirements: vehicle, photo ID, and clear title only — no credit check mentioned
Five Fort Worth-area locations within roughly 15 miles of each other
Publicly posts OCCC-required fee schedules for both single and multiple installment loan types
Licensed and regulated by the Texas Office of Consumer Credit Commissioner
Multiple payment methods accepted: cash, check, debit card, and Western Union
Founded 1990 — over 35 years of operating history in title lending
cons: Vehicle title is pledged as collateral — default risks repossession of your car
Title loan APRs are typically triple digits, among the highest-cost consumer credit products available
In-person application only — no fully online application process mentioned on the site
Limited hours: closed Sundays, weekday locations close at 6 PM
Texas-only footprint visible on the site — limited geographic availability
best_for: Texas vehicle owners who hold a clear, lien-free title and need emergency cash quickly
Borrowers who need more than $1,000 and cannot qualify for personal loans or credit union products
DFW-area residents who prefer in-person service with multiple nearby branch options
People who have exhausted lower-cost alternatives and have a concrete short-term repayment plan
diagnosis: LoanStar Title Loans is best for DFW-area vehicle owners with a clear title who need emergency cash above what payday lenders offer and have no access to lower-cost credit. The central caveat is that title loans are among the most expensive consumer credit products available, and missing a payment puts the borrower's vehicle — often their primary asset — at immediate repossession risk.
services: Auto title loans (car title loans) up to $10,000
Single installment title loan repayment structure
Multiple installment title loan repayment structure
In-person loan applications at Fort Worth and DFW-area branches
